perturbation.original_text: As used herein, the terms “Protected Critical Infrastructure Information” or “PCII” includes information that is not customarily in the public domain and relates to the security of critical infrastructure or protected systems and includes information regarding systems, facilities, or operational security, or that is proprietary, business sensitive, or which might be used to identify a person who submits such information to a federal agency, such as the Department of Homeland Security.
perturbation.changed_text: As used herein, the terms “Protected Critical Infrastructure Information” or “PCII” is strictly limited to documents formally designated as PCII by the Department of Homeland Security under the Critical Infrastructure Information Act of 2002, and for which the TJPA has received explicit written designation.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'PCII' is broad, including information 'related to the security of critical infrastructure,' or 'proprietary, business sensitive' information. The modified definition restricts 'PCII' to information formally designated by DHS under a specific act, requiring explicit written designation for the TJPA. This creates an in-text contradiction in Section 2. Non-Disclosure Of Confidential Information, SSI, Or PCII, as documents that were previously considered PCII under the previous, broad definition, are now not considered so, and therefore, are no longer required to be disclosed, as the NDA agreement dictates.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure Of Confidential Information, SSI, Or PCII. Confidential Information, SSI, and PCII shall be held and treated in the strictest confidence in a manner designed to prevent the unauthorized disclosure to third parties. Confidential Information, SSI, and PCII may appear in records in written or electronic form, including but not limited to, drawings, photographs, schematics, plans, memorandums, reports, emails, videos, tape recordings, and Powerpoint presentations. Confidential Information, SSI, and/or PCII shall not be disclosed to third parties without the express written permission of the Executive Director of the TJPA or her designee; and if such permission is granted, then said information will be provided only upon the execution of a Non- Disclosure Agreement by that third party. If at any time Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII is disclosed in violation of this Agreement, including but not limited to any loss, theft, misuse, misplacement, unauthorized disclosure, or other security violation that the Designated Individual has knowledge of (whether or not he/she is personally involved), the Designated Individual shall immediately give the TJPA written notice of that fact and a detailed account of the circumstances regarding such disclosure. The Designated Individual’s anonymity shall be kept to the extent possible when reporting security violations.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines PCII broadly, encompassing sensitive information related to infrastructure security or proprietary business data. The changed text narrows this definition to only information formally designated as PCII by the Department of Homeland Security under a specific act, requiring explicit written designation for the TJPA. This creates a contradiction because Section 2, Non-Disclosure Of Confidential Information, SSI, Or PCII, mandates strict confidentiality for PCII. Under the original definition, a wider range of documents would have been subject to this non-disclosure requirement. However, under the modified definition, many of those documents would no longer be considered PCII, and therefore, not subject to the same strict confidentiality requirements outlined in Section 2. This constitutes a direct conflict, leading to potential uncertainty about which documents are covered by the non-disclosure agreement, making it a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Designated Individual shall not alter or remove the protective markings of “Confidential Information,” “Sensitive Security Information,” or “Protected Critical Infrastructure Information,” which may appear on documents that the Designated Indiv idual reviews.
perturbation.changed_text: The Designated Individual is encouraged to challenge and question the protective markings of “Confidential Information,” “Sensitive Security Information,” or “Protected Critical Infrastructure Information,” if the individual believes it to be mislabeled. If the Designated Individual believes it to be mislabeled, the Designated Individual is encouraged to petition the TJPA for a review of the markings
perturbation.explanation: The original text prohibits altering or removing protective markings. The modified text encourages questioning and petitioning for review if markings are believed to be incorrect, implicitly allowing for a challenge. This creates a contradiction with the original requirement to treat all documents with protective markings as such, creating a contradiction in Section 3, as to how Confidential Information is handled
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use and Handling of Information. Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII so marked shall be used solely for the purpose of preparing and submitting a bid for a portion of the wor k at the Project and/or performing work at the Project, and shall not be used for any other purpose without the express written consent by the Executive Director of the TJPA or her designee. Confidential Information, SSI, and PCII shall be handled and safe guarded by the Designated Individual in a manner that affords sufficient protection to prevent the unauthorized disclosure of or access to such information, consistent with the laws, regulations, or directives applicable to the specific categories of infor mation. The Designated Individual shall not alter or remove the protective markings of “Confidential Information,” “Sensitive Security Information,” or “Protected Critical Infrastructure Information,” which may appear on documents that the Designated Indiv idual reviews. Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII may be used for the sole purpose of preparing and submitting a bid for a portion of the work at the Project and/or performing work at the Project. If the Designated Individual must use Confidential In formation, SSI, or PCII to create derivative documents for the purpose of preparing and submitting a bid for a portion of the work and/or performing work at the Project, the appropriate protective marking shall be carried forward to the derivative document s. All derivative documents shall be protected in the same manner as the original documents.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibits altering or removing protective markings. The changed text encourages questioning and petitioning for review if markings are believed to be incorrect, implicitly allowing for a challenge or change. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states 'The Designated Individual shall not alter or remove the protective markings'. This is a strong contradiction because it provides conflicting instructions on how to handle confidential information markings, potentially leading to unauthorized disclosure if the 'Designated Individual' misinterprets the permission to challenge as permission to alter.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the earlier occurrence of: (1) the TJPA’s written request; (2) ten (10) business days after Final Completion of the Project (or for non -successful Bidders, within ten (10) business days after the award of the Trade Work subcontract); (3) upon th e conclusion of the Designated Individual’s duties, association, or support to the Contractor in connection with the Project; or (4) upon the determination that the Designated Individual’s official duties do not require further access to Confidential Infor mation, SSI, or PCII, any and all documents containing Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII in the possession of the Designated Individual shall be promptly destroyed , or, if requested by the TJPA, returned to the TJPA .
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the earlier occurrence of: (1) the TJPA’s written request; (2) ten (10) business days after Final Completion of the Project (or for non -successful Bidders, within ten (10) business days after the award of the Trade Work subcontract); (3) upon th e conclusion of the Designated Individual’s duties, association, or support to the Contractor in connection with the Project; or (4) upon the determination that the Designated Individual’s official duties do not require further access to Confidential Infor mation, SSI, or PCII, the Designated Individual may retain copies of all documents containing Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII for archival purposes. The Designated Individual should make a reasonable effort to secure these documents.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the destruction or return of all documents containing Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII upon specific events. The modified text allows the Designated Individual to retain copies for 'archival purposes,' only requiring a 'reasonable effort' to secure them. This contradicts the mandatory destruction/return requirement in the original, and creates an in-text contradiction in section 7. Violations and Remedies, as there is no clear protocol to manage the unsecure documents for the purpose of remedies and violations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Violations And Remedies. Unauthorized Disclosure: If a Contractor or any Designated Individual knows or has a reasonable belief that documents subject to the Specification have been or may be disclosed to an unauthorized person(s), the Contractor and/or Designated Individual shall imme diately notify the TJPA. The TJPA shall make the final determination as to whether a violation of the Specification occurred and the course of action to address the violation. Remedies: Any Contractor or Designated Individual that violates the Specificat ion with respect to Confidential Information, SSI and/or PCII shall, at the TJPA’s election, be subject to any of the following: (1) written reprimand of the Contractor and/or Designated Individual; (2) termination of the Contractor or Designated Individual’s access to Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII; (3) directive to the Contractor to immediately suspended the Designated Individual from performing any further work relating to the Project; (4) if a contract has been awarded, treatment of the unautho rized disclosure of Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII as a material breach of the contract, warranting the termination of the contract for cause; (5) commencement of legal proceedings for any other legal rights and remedies, including injunctive relie f and monetary damages arising from the breach of contract; (6) reporting of any violation related to SSI or PCII to the Department of Homeland Security, the Transportation Security Administration, or the Department of Transportation (these agencies may also independently assess penalties and/or take corrective action as provided under Federal law, including 49 C.F.R. §§ 15.17 and 1520.17); (7) disqualification of a Contractor from bidding on any work for the Project; and/or (8) written directive to retriev e all Confidential Information, SSI, and/or PCII disclosed to unauthorized persons at Contractor’s expense and to cease future unauthorized disclosure. If legal proceedings to enforce any remedy set forth under this paragraph are commenced by the TJPA, the prevailing party in such legal action shall be entitled to reasonable attorney’s fees and costs . Furthermore, Contractor agrees to indemnify the TJPA for any expenses or damages (including attorney’s fees or costs) incurred by the TJPA arising out of or relating to the Contractor and/or Designated Individual’s unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information, SSI, and/or PCII.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the destruction or return of confidential documents, while the changed text allows the Designated Individual to retain copies for archival purposes with only a 'reasonable effort' to secure them. This directly contradicts the original mandatory destruction/return requirement. This introduces an in-text contradiction with the 'Violations And Remedies' section, as there is no clear protocol to manage unsecure documents, which contradicts the requirement for immediate notification of unauthorized disclosure and the subsequent remedies for violations.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations under this Agreement shall be perpetual (unless otherwise provided for herein) or until such time as the information is no longer considered Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII and a written notice to that fact is provided to the Designa ted Individual and/or the Contractor by the Executive Director of the TJPA.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations under this Agreement shall expire one (1) year after the Final Completion of the Project, regardless of whether the information is still considered Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII, unless explicitly renewed in writing by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the confidentiality obligations are perpetual. The modified text limits the obligations to one year after project completion, unless renewed. This directly contradicts the perpetual nature of the original agreement, leading to a clear temporal contradiction. It is now contradicting the section 6, concerning Retention Limitations and Return of Information, which is now outdated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Retention Limitations; Return Of Information. Upon the earlier occurrence of: (1) the TJPA’s written request; (2) ten (10) business days after Final Completion of the Project (or for non -successful Bidders, within ten (10) business days after the award of the Trade Work subcontract); (3) upon th e conclusion of the Designated Individual’s duties, association, or support to the Contractor in connection with the Project; or (4) upon the determination that the Designated Individual’s official duties do not require further access to Confidential Infor mation, SSI, or PCII, any and all documents containing Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII in the possession of the Designated Individual shall be promptly destroyed , or, if requested by the TJPA, returned to the TJPA . Upon returning or destroying the a forementioned documents, the Designated Individual shall sign the Affidavit of Return or Destruction in the form set forth in Section 01 35 70/AT2. The Designated Individual understands and acknowledges that for documents returned to the TJPA, it is within the TJPA’s sole discretion to determine whether to destroy or maintain any returned documents. Notwithstanding the foregoing, if required by law, the Designated Individual may retain documents containing Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII only if: (1) the TJPA is notified in writing of such retention; and (2) the Designated Individual continues to abide by the requirements of this Agreement with respect to protection of Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulates that confidentiality obligations are perpetual. The changed text limits these obligations to one year after project completion, unless renewed. This creates a direct temporal contradiction with the 'Retention Limitations; Return Of Information' section, which outlines procedures for handling confidential information without any time limitations that expire one year after the Final Completion of the Project, as the changed text states. Therefore, the modified text creates a strong in-text contradiction.
